1→"""Shell activation script generation for worktree environments. 2→ 3→This module provides utilities for generating shell scripts that activate 4→worktree environments by setting up virtual environments and loading .env files. 5→""" 6→ 7→import shlex 8→from collections.abc import Sequence 9→from dataclasses import dataclass 10→from pathlib import Path 11→ 12→import click 13→ 14→from erk.core.display_utils import copy_to_clipboard_osc52 15→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 16→ 17→ 18→@dataclass(frozen=True) 19→class ActivationConfig: 20→ """Configuration for activation instructions. 21→ 22→ Replaces the combinatorial ActivationMode Literal with composable flags. 23→ All fields required - use factory functions for convenience. 24→ 25→ Attributes: 26→ implement: If True, append `erk implement` to the source command. 27→ False means activate-only mode (just source the script). 28→ dangerous: If True, include --dangerous flag (skip permission prompts). 29→ docker: If True, include --docker flag (filesystem isolation). 30→ codespace: If not None, include --codespace flag for codespace isolation.

Excellent! Now let me create a comprehensive summary. Let me compile the full report with exact line numbers and file paths:
## Summary
I've completed a thorough search of the erk codebase for all uses of the `--dangerous` flag. Here are my findings:
### 1. WHERE IS `--dangerous` DEFINED AS A CLI OPTION?
The `--dangerous` flag is defined in **7 Click commands**:
1. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/implement_shared.py:91-97`** - Decorator function `implement_common_options` that applies common options to implement commands - Definition spans lines 91-97 with `-d` shortcut on line 92 - Help text: "Skip permission prompts by passing --dangerously-skip-permissions to Claude"
2. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/learn/learn_cmd.py:70-75`** - Learn command for extracting insights from plan implementations - Definition spans lines 70-75 with `-d` shortcut on line 71 - Help text: "Launch Claude with --dangerously-skip-permissions (skip all permission prompts)"
3. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/address_cmd.py:14-20`** - Address PR review comments command - Definition spans lines 14-20 with `-d` shortcut on line 16 - Help text: "Acknowledge that this command invokes Claude with --dangerously-skip-permissions."
4. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rebase_cmd.py:14-20`** - Rebase PR onto base branch command - Definition spans lines 14-20 with `-d` shortcut on line 16 - Help text: "Acknowledge that this command invokes Claude with --dangerously-skip-permissions."
5. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/reconcile_with_remote_cmd.py:14-20`** - Reconcile branch with remote command - Definition spans lines 14-20 with `-d` shortcut on line 16 - Help text: "Acknowledge that this command invokes Claude with --dangerously-skip-permissions."
6. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/create_cmd.py:52-57`** - Branch create command - Definition spans lines 52-57 with `-d` shortcut on line 53 - Help text: "Include --dangerous flag to skip permission prompts during implementation"
7. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/objective/plan_cmd.py:434-440`** - Create plan from objective command - Definition spans lines 434-440 with `-d` shortcut on line 436 - Help text: "Allow dangerous permissions by passing --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions to Claude"
8. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/codespace/run/objective/plan_cmd.py:13-19`** - Run objective plan remotely on codespace - Definition spans lines 13-19 with `-d` shortcut on line 14 - Help text: "Allow dangerous permissions by passing --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions to Claude"
### 2. WHAT DOES IT DO?
The `--dangerous` flag serves to: - Skip permission prompts when executing Claude Code commands - Pass `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag to Claude CLI - Act as an acknowledgment that the user understands the implications of skipping safety prompts - Enable automated/non-interactive modes that would otherwise be blocked by permission prompts
### 3. HOW IS IT USED IN IMPLEMENTATION?
The dangerous flag is processed in these key places:
**`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/implement_shared.py:220-246`** - Builds Claude arguments: - Line 222: `if dangerous: args.append("--dangerously-skip-permissions")` - Line 243: `if dangerous: cmd += " --dangerously-skip-permissions"`
**`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/activation.py:80-81`** - Adds to activation script: - Line 80: `if config.dangerous: parts.append("--dangerous")`
**`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/implement.py:202`** - Passed to interactive mode execution
**`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/output.py:132,156`** - Used in stream command execution
### 4. DOES IT ALREADY HAVE A `-d` SHORTCUT?
**YES - ALL 8 Click command definitions already have the `-d` shortcut:**
Every single `--dangerous` flag definition includes `-d` as the short form: ```python @click.option( "-d", "--dangerous", is_flag=True, help="..." ) ```
This pattern is consistent across all commands with no exceptions.
